method.boundary: A first-viewport visual review plus automated rendered-box evidence. It is not usability research, accessibility conformance, functional acceptance, or a universal model ranking.

results.observation: Readable card grid and a real two-column-to-inbox mobile change, but visually generic and missing the main landmark.
Strong dark operations language and compact mobile inbox; duplicate hidden responsive records and IDs weaken component logic.
The clearest dense desktop comparison and a convincing mobile card transformation; hidden duplicate records and a short action-label issue remain.
Distinct topographic treatment, but the large introduction and summary defer the actual task queue below the first mobile viewport.
Editorial command-center direction is distinctive, but desktop right-rail content collides and mobile columns overlap into an unreadable stack.
Best balanced rescue result: dense comparison remains readable, semantic states stay distinct, and mobile reorders cleanly without collisions.
Mobile horizontal replacement is readable, but desktop puts a narrow vertical line in an oversized empty panel and fails the required vertical hook contract.
Elegant dark editorial system from the one-time infrastructure retry, but the evaluated vertical element does not become the required desktop-to-mobile writing-mode pair.
Strong newspaper-like identity and clean mobile hierarchy; visible vertical accents exist, but the required hook fails its writing-mode contract.
Readable warm editorial cards, but no convincing vertical composition is visible and both viewports exhibit horizontal overflow.
The mobile horizontal version becomes readable, but desktop vertical type collides directly with the headline and body copy.
Best vertical-type concept and the clearest horizontal mobile replacement; rendered-box checks still found clipped visible text and one short-label issue.
Desktop pages are basic but understandable; mobile home is covered by an open dark menu and the catalog header becomes a large broken black band.
Mature curatorial identity across three pages; catalog mobile clips the brand to one character and root design tokens drift between pages.
Strongest visually coherent bookstore set: editorial shelf, catalog, and detail compositions retain one identity on both viewports, despite machine-detected token drift.
Warm, usable, and visibly coherent on all six captures; machine checks still find token/shell drift, non-zh-Hant lang values, and clipped cover text.
Distinct fold-book identity and consistent route composition; oversized mobile headings add scrolling cost and machine checks find token/shell drift plus a short-label issue.
Memorable striped-book identity, but catalog branding clips and the detail-cover title overlaps heavily on desktop and mobile; shell styles drift across pages.
